records.ety: [Perhaps orig. a drinking company, fr. OF. beve (cf. It. beva) a drink, beverage; then, perh., a company in general, esp. of ladies; and last applied by sportsmen to larks, quails, etc. See Beverage.]

defs: A company; an assembly or collection of persons, especially of ladies.
quotes: What a bevy of beaten slaves have we here !

defs: A flock of birds, especially quails or larks; also, a herd of roes.
